hints.to put: That would be 'ponere' — 'Possunt' comes from 'posse' (to be able)
hints.to demand: That would be 'poscere' — 'Possunt' comes from 'posse' (to be able)
hints.to possess: That would be 'possidere' — 'Possunt' comes from 'posse' (to be able)

steps.reveal: Possunt = they are able / they can

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'possint' — 'possunt' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'potuerunt' — 'possunt' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'poterunt' — 'possunt' is present indicative

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the parts of 'ponere' (to put/place)
hints.posco, poscere, poposci, —: These are the parts of 'poscere' (to demand)
hints.possideo, possidere, possedi, possessum: These are the parts of 'possidere' (to possess)

hints.to put: That would be 'ponere' — 'posse' is the infinitive of 'possum' (to be able)
hints.to demand: That would be 'poscere' — 'posse' means 'to be able'
hints.to drink: That would be 'potare' — 'posse' is the infinitive of 'possum'

steps.reveal: posse = to be able (infinitive in indirect statement with 'videntur')

hints.present indicative: 'posse' is an infinitive, not a finite verb — it lacks personal endings
hints.perfect infinitive: Perfect infinitive would be 'potuisse' — 'posse' is present infinitive
hints.future infinitive: Future infinitive is not standard for 'posse' — 'posse' is present infinitive

hints.pono, ponere, posui, positum: These are the parts of 'ponere' (to put/place)
hints.poto, potare, potavi, potatum: These are the parts of 'potare' (to drink)
hints.posco, poscere, poposci, —: These are the parts of 'poscere' (to demand)

hints.to see: 'Videre' active means 'to see', but 'videntur' is passive — 'videri' means 'to seem'
hints.to come: That would be 'venire' — 'videntur' comes from 'videri' (to seem)
hints.to conquer: That would be 'vincere' — 'videntur' comes from 'videri' (to seem)

steps.reveal: videntur = they seem (passive of 'video' = to seem)

hints.present subjunctive passive: Present subjunctive passive would be 'videantur' — 'videntur' is present indicative
hints.perfect indicative passive: Perfect passive would be 'visi sunt' — 'videntur' is present indicative passive
hints.imperfect indicative passive: Imperfect passive would be 'videbantur' — 'videntur' is present indicative

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come)
hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.vivo, vivere, vixi, victum: These are the parts of 'vivere' (to live)

translation: They can because they seem to be able
